cards.back: A flexible and iterative approach to project management that emphasizes collaboration, flexibility, and rapid delivery
A time-boxed iteration in an Agile project, typically lasting 1-4 weeks, during which a set of tasks is completed
A set of principles and practices used in Agile projects, emphasizing teamwork, accountability, and iterative progress toward well-defined goals
A role in a Scrum team responsible for defining and prioritizing the product backlog, ensuring the team has a clear understanding of the project's goals and objectives
A list of features or tasks that need to be completed in a project, prioritized and ordered based on importance and urgency
